questionHandled: The user reported that the wiki log was visible inside the web wiki even though it should stay out of the navigable wiki surface.

sourcesRead.reason: Input event created from the mini UI with the user's request and the active document path.
Checked the stable static wiki reader behavior and its exclusion list.
Checked how the experimental mini UI discovers and serves documents.
Updated the prototype note so the mini UI document boundary is explicit.

authoritativeDestination.reason: The durable wiki reader already excludes wiki/log.md. The defect was in the experimental mini UI document set.

factsObserved: The event selected wiki/log.md as the active mini UI document.
scripts/render-wiki.mjs excludes log.md from generated wiki reader pages.
experiments/wiki-agent-orchestration/mini-ui/server.mjs listed all Markdown files under wiki/ except skipped directories, so wiki/log.md was still visible in the mini UI.
The requested behavior is a UI/document-discovery correction, not a new wiki governance decision.
assumptions: wiki/log.md should remain in the repository as a source-side change register.
The mini UI should mirror the reader-visible wiki surface unless a future spec says otherwise.
gapsOrContradictions: The mini UI and the stable static reader had two separate exclusion mechanisms. This is acceptable for the prototype, but a future implementation should centralize reader visibility rules.

proposedChange.summary: Exclude wiki/log.md from the experimental mini UI document set and document that the log is not a navigable wiki page.
proposedChange.content: Add log.md to the wiki source skipFiles list, apply the same skip rule when serving documents by path, and note the exclusion in the mini UI README.

nonAutoApplyReason: The user asked to fix the issue directly in the active session, and the change is limited to the experimental mini UI plus its README note.
nextQuestions: Should reader-visible document rules become a shared helper if the web wiki prototype grows beyond the current experiment?
